About us Access and Outreach Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Academic Visitor Programme Vacancies Contact us Oscola Baroness Ruth Deech, DBE KC(Hon) Chairman, Bar Standards Board, 2009-14. Crossbench Peer St Anne's College Email Biography Ruth Deech was the first Independent Adjudicator for Higher Education 2004-8. She was Principal of St. Anne's College from 1991 to 2004 and former Fellow and Tutor in Law of that college, specialising in Family and Property Law. Hon. LLD Strathclyde University. From 1994-2002 she was Chairman of the UK Human Fertilisation and Embryology Authority , established by legislation in 1990 to consider ethical questions arising from advances in reproductive medicine, and to regulate research and treatment of infertility. She wrote a report Women Doctors:Making a Difference for the DH in 2009. She was a member of the Committee of Inquiry into Equal Opportunities on the Bar Vocational Course 1993-4, and chair of the Bar Standards Board 2009-14. She was a Governor of the BBC from 2002-6, a Rhodes Trustee 1996-06, and is a Bencher of the Inner Temple. She was a member of the Executive Committee of the International Society of Family Law, a member of the Editorial Board of Child and Family Law Quarterly, an Honorary Fellow of the Society for Advanced Legal Studies and an Honorary Member of the SLS. In 2005 she was appointed a Crossbench Peer and chaired the Lords Select Committee on the Equality Act 2010 and Disability. In 2023 she was appointed Chair of the House of Lords Appointment Commission. pages.title: Access & Outreach | Faculty of Law
pages.meta_description: The Oxford Law Faculty is committed to widening its intake of students from all backgrounds. We provide a network of support systems and outreach schemes
pages.text_excerpt: Access & Outreach | Faculty of Law Skip to main content Access & Outreach Back to faculty site Menu Events News Open Days People The Oxford Law Faculty is committed to widening its intake of students from all backgrounds. We provide a network of support systems and outreach schemes targeted at applicants from low income backgrounds, first generation students, and schools with a low history of progression to Oxford. Welcome! Welcome to our Access & Outreach homepage. The Oxford Law Faculty is committed to widening its intake of students from all backgrounds. We provide a network of support systems and outreach schemes targeted at applicants from low income backgrounds, first generation students, and schools with a low history of progression to Oxford. The Faculty of Law is committed to attracting the strongest students to study, regardless of a candidate’s background. In order to achieve this aim, we host range of activities designed to inform, support and encourage applications from widest possible pool. Within the Faculty we offer outreach programmes such our Law Taster Days, Summer School and Mock Trials while working with the University to offer other programmes such as UNIQ, Opportunity Oxford and the University Open Days. The University of Oxford now offers the opportunity for schools and colleges to develop an ongoing relationship with Colleges at the University to simplify communication between UK Schools and the University as an ongoing commitment to engage in schools. The programme pairs colleges with specific UK regional Local Authorities, to allow the University to get to know schools and colleges in specific regions better, but also give schools and colleges a more personal and direct way of staying in touch with the University. For further information on pairing please visit the Regional Outreach pages on the University of Oxford website. University Access and Outreach The University of Oxford offers a range of outreach initiatives relating to studying Law. UNIQ UNIQ is the University of Oxford’s access programme for UK state school students in their first year of further education. UNIQ Astrophoria Foundation Year The Astrophoria Foundation Year is a one-year programme for UK state school students with strong academic potential who have experienced significant disadvantage. It offers a supported route to undergraduate study at Oxford. Astrophoria Foundation Year Opportunity Oxford Opportunity Oxford is an academic transition programme designed to support talented UK offer holders from under-represented backgrounds as they move from school or college to undergraduate study at Oxford. Opportunity Oxford Oxplore Oxplore is an engaging digital resource from the University of Oxford which aims to challenge those from 11 to 18 years with debates and ideas that go beyond what is covered in the classroom. Oxplore Oxford Open Days The University of Oxford's 2026 undergraduate Open Days will be held on 1 and 2 July and 18 September. Oxford Open Days Faculty Access and Outreach The Law Faculty also engages in the following initiatives to further support our recruitment aims. Target Oxbridge Target Oxbridge aims to help black African and Caribbean students and students of mixed race with black African and Caribbean heritage increase their chances of getting into the Universities of Oxford or Cambridge. Target Oxbridge ExpLaw Summer School This 2 day programme is run by the Faculty of Law and supported by Freshfields. Students take part in tutorials, lectures and visit different colleges. ExpLaw prioritises state school students from backgrounds that are under-represented at Oxford. ExpLaw Insight Day The Insight Day is for Year 12 students from underrepresented backgrounds. This Faculty of Law event supported by Blackstone Chambers is designed to introduce prospective students to legal study and careers. pages.title: Advanced Programme on Competition Law and the Digital
pages.h1: Advanced Programme on Competition Law and the Digital Economy
pages.meta_description: Who is the programme for? The programme is designed to enrich the knowledge of solicitors and barristers, in-house counsel, consultants, industry experts
headings: h2: Who is the programme for?
h2: What the programme will cover
h2: What past participants have said:
pages.text_excerpt: Advanced Programme on Competition Law and the Digital Skip to main content Professional Development Programmes Back to faculty site Menu About Professional Development Programmes Advanced Programme on AI Law and Policy Advanced Programme on Competition Law in the Digital Economy Advanced Programme on Regulatory Systems Custom Professional Development Programmes Terms and Conditions Advanced Programme on Competition Law and the Digital Economy Course overview Who is the programme for? Programme Faculty and Contributors Application and Payment Overview In the rapidly evolving digital economy, navigating the legal and regulatory aspects issues is a complex task. Gaining access to the latest research, perspectives and people at the heart of this dynamic sector is essential. The Advanced Programme on Competition Law and the Digital Economy has been designed to deepen the understanding of digital market dynamics, the growing power of key industry players, current enforcement, and regulatory responses. Sessions will offer a deep dive into the rise of the platform economy, the role of data analytics and artificial intelligence, practical enforcement and compliance considerations. Join us and engage in stimulating cross-functional discussion with practitioners, in-house lawyers, consultants, industry experts, scholars and enforcement officials. If you are interested in shaping the future of the platform economy to maintain growth and innovation within digital markets this is the programme for you. Download the brochure Course in brief Course length 2.5 days Participants will access the latest research and practical insights from our faculty, complemented by expert speakers to create an immersive learning environment, with knowledge that can be applied to real-world challenges in this area. Dates: 22 - 24 June 2026 Location: Pembroke College Available places: c.40 Reserve your space Contact: pdp@law.ox.ac.uk Cost: £3,500 plus accommodation (£350), including all meals and formal college dinner with keynote speaker Download the brochure Who is the programme for? The programme is designed to enrich the knowledge of solicitors and barristers, in-house counsel, consultants, industry experts, directors, and enforcers interested in the law, economics and policy that govern competition in digital markets. What the programme will cover Digital Market dynamics Market definition Platforms and Ecosystems’ power Abuse of dominance - exploitation Algorithmic collusion and alignment Cooperation and contractual restrictions The EU Digital Markets Act The EU Digital Services Act The UK Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act Merger control Innovation Complaints and private enforcement Compliance and risk mitigation Generative Artificial Intelligence and competition Benefits to you Benefits to your organisation Cutting edge review of recent developments and enforcement trajectory Discussion of risks, opportunities, and compliance implications for companies across the value chain Gain perspectives from leading experts from some of the most influential law firms, economic consultancies, and organisations active in this dynamic sector Network with peers in an exclusive, small-group setting Contribute towards your annual Continued Professional Development requirement Join the Faculty of Law professional programmes alumni for life Introduce enriched understanding within your organisation of the scope and limits of competition law and regulation, business, risk and compliance implications Cascade learning through your team and into the wider organisation Enhance your knowledge and expertise with practical insights your organisation can start applying immediately Make deeper cross-organisational connections What past participants have said: The panel discussions were exceptionally well-moderated, and the range of views were great, comprising both tech lawyers and writers of the DMA. The environment was also conducive to occasional questions, which enhanc

h3: Our blogs are written by individual contributors and so consist of individual opinions and viewpoints which are not necessarily the views of either the Faculty of Law or of the University of Oxford.
pages.text_excerpt: Explore our blogs | Oxford Law Blogs Skip to main content Main navigation Law Faculty website Search Leave this field blank Explore our blogs Discover fresh opinions, informed commentary and academic debate on a wide range of legal issues. Blogs at the Faculty of Law Border Criminologies Border Criminologies is an international network of researchers, practitioners, and those who have experienced border control. Read our blog for current research and reviews. View blogs Centre for Criminology Blog The Centre for Criminology Blog allows Criminology students to write about their research and on current topics of interest in key areas of Criminology. View blogs Death Penalty Research Unit Blog The Death Penalty Research Unit Blog explores the retention, administration and politics of the death penalty worldwide, including its diverse application and impact on communities. View blogs Family and Medical Law On the Family and Medical Law Blog, academic postholders, postdoctoral researchers and research students share fresh insights on recent cases and changes in the law, and new findings from their research. View blogs Frontiers of Socio-Legal Studies The Frontiers of Socio-Legal Studies Blog provides a discussion space for scholars at all career stages and from all over the world to engage in debate about issues at the forefront of the field. Frontiers of Socio-Legal Studies Housing After Grenfell Blog The Housing After Grenfell Blog provides a forum for discussion and debate about housing and law-related matters. View blogs Oxford Business Law Blog The Oxford Business Law Blog is a forum for the exchange of ideas and the reporting of new developments in all aspects of business law. View blogs Oxford Human Rights Hub The Oxford Human Rights Hub strives to build a global community around human rights issues by enhancing our understanding of law and practice. Read our blog for reflections on the latest developments. Oxford Human Rights Hub Oxford University Undergraduate Law Journal Blog This is Oxford's sole publication dedicated to undergraduate legal writing, edited and staffed entirely by students. View blogs Property Law Blog The Property Law Blog provides a forum for discussion and debate about property law. We welcome submissions on a range of topics and in a range of formats. View blogs Latest blogs 1 May 2026 Moving Toward Shared Responsibility: How the EU’s CSDDD and Omnibus I Reimagine Contracting for Human Rights and Environmental Due Diligence by: Sarah Dadush, Daniel Schönfelder 1 May 2026 'The 28th Regime: An Effective Legal Architecture for Innovation in Europe?' – ECGI-Bocconi-LawFin Workshop Series on 'The Law and Finance of Private Equity and Venture Capital' by: Luca Enriques, Casimiro A Nigro 1 May 2026 The permanently provisional: Living on a militarised border in south Lebanon by: Hucen Sleiman 30 April 2026 Insolvency as a Blind Spot in the UK Anti-Money Laundering Regime by: Oriana Casasola, Ilaria Zavoli 30 April 2026 Lessons from the Convergence of Corporate Restructurings by: Robert W. Miller 29 April 2026 EU Inc: A Flexible but Incomplete Blueprint for European Startup Finance by: Giovanni Strampelli Our blogs are written by individual contributors and so consist of individual opinions and viewpoints which are not necessarily the views of either the Faculty of Law or of the University of Oxford. pages.text_excerpt: Development and Alumni Engagement | Faculty of Law Skip to main content Oxford Law Alumni and Supporters Back to faculty site Menu Philanthropic Priorities and Gift Opportunities Alumni Benefits and Professional Development Contact Us Development and Alumni Engagement Our Development and Alumni Engagement Office helps alumni and supporters find exciting opportunities to connect with and give back to Oxford Law. We warmly welcome exploratory conversations around gifting opportunities. Contact Us To start a conversation about making a donation or enquire about alumni events, volunteering, or anything else, please email development@law.ox.ac.uk or call (+44) 01865 611542. Meet Our Team Craig Edwards Head of Development Stuart Watson Development and Alumni Engagement Coordinator Katie Foxton Development Officer Useful links for alumni My Oxford Online : Register to receive news, updates, and event invitations by registering and updating your contact details. Oxford University Careers Service : Visit to log in to CareerConnect and access job vacancy boards, careers fairs, webinars, and private career guidance appointments. Oxford Alumni – Your Certificates: Visit for guidance on how to access electronic degree certificates and transcripts, or order paper copies.
